--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,61 +6,81 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a880
 project: spatial-nav
-title: 'Handle dynamic FocusScope lifecycle: unmount, virtualization, batch registration'
+title: 'Dynamic FocusScope lifecycle: layer-scoped fallback, virtualization, batch'
 ---
 ## What

-Handle edge cases where FocusScopes mount/unmount dynamically — virtualized lists, deleted entities, inspector field changes. The core spatial registry from cards 1-2 handles the happy path (mount → register, unmount → unregister). This card handles the hard cases.
+Handle edge cases where FocusScopes mount/unmount dynamically — virtualized lists, deleted entities, inspector field changes. The core spatial registry from cards 1–2 handles the happy path (mount → register, unmount → unregister). This card handles the hard cases — all while respecting the hard layer boundary from card `01KNQXXF5W7G4JP73C6ZCMKYKX`.
+
+### Case 1: Focused scope unmounts (fallback within layer)
+
+When the focused entity is deleted or scrolled out of the virtual viewport, its FocusScope unmounts and calls `spatial_unregister`. Now `navigate()` or any subsequent `focus-changed` emit has no origin rect.

-### Case 1: Focused scope unmounts
+**Solution**: the state manager watches for the unregister of a focused key. When that happens, it computes a fallback **within the same layer** the unregistered entry lived in:

-When the focused entity is deleted or scrolled out of the virtual viewport, its FocusScope unmounts and calls `spatial_unregister`. Now `navigate()` can't find the origin rect.
+1. First try: pick the nearest remaining entry to the unregistered entry's last rect (using the same beam/score logic, omnidirectional — closest in any direction).
+2. If the layer is now empty of entries, walk up to the parent layer and use its `last_focused`.
+3. If no parent (layer was a window root), emit `focus-changed` with `next_key: None` — window has no focus until a user gesture restores it.

-**Solution**: `navigate()` checks if the focused moniker exists in the registry. If not, it falls back:
-- Return `First` direction result (top-left-most in active layer) as the new focus target
-- React detects the stale focus via the response and calls `setFocus(fallback)`
+Fallback never crosses into another window's layer forest, even if another window has entries. The user's attention is tied to the window they're in; teleporting their focus to a different window would be disorienting.

-Add a `navigate()` return type that distinguishes `Found(moniker)` from `FallbackToFirst(moniker)` so React can log the recovery.
+Add a return type to the internal resolution so logs/telemetry can distinguish `Found`, `FallbackNearestInLayer`, `FallbackParentLayer`, `NoFocus`.

 ### Case 2: Virtualized lists

 Only visible cards have mounted FocusScopes. `nav.down` past the last visible card finds nothing below in the registry. The virtualizer must scroll to reveal the next card before spatial nav can resolve.

-**Solution — placeholder rects**: The virtualizer registers **estimated rects** for off-screen items via `spatial_register(moniker, estimated_rect, layer)`. These are computed from item index × estimated height. When nav lands on a placeholder:
+**Solution — placeholder rects in the same layer**: The virtualizer registers **estimated rects** for off-screen items via `spatial_register_batch` with their `layer_key` set to the virtualizer's enclosing layer (usually the window root). These are computed from item index × estimated height. When nav lands on a placeholder:
 1. Rust returns the placeholder's moniker
 2. React calls `setFocus(moniker)` which tells the virtualizer to scroll-to-item
-3. Virtualizer scrolls, real FocusScope mounts, calls `spatial_register` with measured rect (overwrites the estimate)
+3. Virtualizer scrolls, real FocusScope mounts, calls `spatial_register` with measured rect (overwrites the estimate) — same `SpatialKey`, so it's an update, not a new entry

-This means `spatial_register` can be called by both FocusScope (measured) and the virtualizer (estimated). The Rust side doesn't distinguish — it's just a rect.
+This means `spatial_register` can be called by both FocusScope (measured) and the virtualizer (estimated). The Rust side doesn't distinguish — it's just a rect update.

-**Where to register placeholders**: `VirtualizedCardList` in `column-view.tsx` knows all task monikers and estimated positions. On mount, it registers estimated rects for all items (not just visible ones). On unmount of the list, it unregisters all. The real FocusScope mounts overwrite estimates with measurements.
+**Where to register placeholders**: `VirtualizedCardList` in `column-view.tsx` knows all task monikers and estimated positions. On mount, it reads its `layer_key` from `useCurrentLayerKey()` and registers estimated rects for all items (not just visible ones), all with that layer_key. On unmount of the list, it unregisters all. The real FocusScope mounts overwrite estimates with measurements by reusing the same SpatialKey (the virtualizer generates it, shares it with the card component via props).

 ### Case 3: Batch registration

 When a virtualizer reveals 20 cards or the board first renders, many FocusScopes mount simultaneously. Each calls `spatial_register` individually — 20 Tauri invokes.

-**Solution**: Add `spatial_register_batch(entries: Vec<(moniker, rect, layer)>)` Tauri command. FocusScope can still use the single-entry version. The virtualizer uses batch for placeholder registration.
+**Solution**: Add `spatial_register_batch(entries: Vec<RegisterEntry>)` Tauri command. FocusScope can still use the single-entry version. The virtualizer uses batch for placeholder registration.
+
+```rust
+struct RegisterEntry {
+    key: SpatialKey,
+    moniker: String,
+    rect: Rect,
+    layer_key: LayerKey,
+}
+```

 ### Subtasks
-- [ ] `navigate()` handles missing focused moniker — falls back to First in active layer
-- [ ] Add placeholder rect registration from VirtualizedCardList for off-screen items
+- [ ] `navigate` or state manager handles missing focused moniker — layer-scoped fallback
+- [ ] Layer-pop fallback walks up parents via `ancestors_of` (never crosses window roots)
+- [ ] Add placeholder rect registration from VirtualizedCardList for off-screen items, scoped to its layer_key
 - [ ] Add `spatial_register_batch` Tauri command for bulk registration
-- [ ] Handle scroll-to-focus flow: nav lands on placeholder → virtualizer scrolls → real mount overwrites
+- [ ] Handle scroll-to-focus flow: nav lands on placeholder → virtualizer scrolls → real mount overwrites same SpatialKey
 - [ ] Add tests for all three cases

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Deleting the focused entity doesn't break navigation — focus moves to a sensible fallback
+- [ ] Deleting the focused entity restores focus within the same layer (nearest remaining entry, or parent layer's `last_focused`)
+- [ ] Deleting the focused entity in window A never moves focus to window B
+- [ ] Deleting the only entry in a layer falls back to parent layer's `last_focused`
 - [ ] `nav.down` past the last visible card in a virtualized list scrolls to and focuses the next card
+- [ ] Virtualizer placeholders use the virtualizer's enclosing `layer_key`, not a special "placeholder" layer
 - [ ] Batch registration works for virtualizer placeholder setup
 - [ ] No stale entries left in registry after component unmount
 - [ ] `cargo test` passes, `pnpm vitest run` passes

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — navigate with missing focused moniker returns fallback
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — navigate returns placeholder moniker when it's the nearest match
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — register overwrites existing entry (placeholder → measured)
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — register_batch adds multiple entries atomically
-- [ ] `column-view.test.tsx` or integration — nav.down past visible area scrolls and focuses next card
+
+- [ ] Rust: unregister focused key → fallback returns nearest remaining entry in same layer
+- [ ] Rust: unregister focused key when layer is now empty → fallback returns parent layer's `last_focused`
+- [ ] Rust: unregister focused key in a root layer with no siblings → `focus_by_window[that_window]` cleared, `focus-changed` with `next_key: None`
+- [ ] Rust: fallback never returns an entry with a different `window_label` than the unregistered entry
+- [ ] Rust: register overwrites existing entry (placeholder → measured rect) for same `SpatialKey`
+- [ ] Rust: `spatial_register_batch` adds multiple entries atomically (single lock acquisition)
+- [ ] `column-view.test.tsx` or integration: nav.down past visible area scrolls and focuses next card
 - [ ] Run `cargo test` and `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all pass

 ## Workflow

-1. **Sibling in same zone.** Pick the nearest remaining entry where `candidate.parent_zone() == lost.parent_zone()` in the same `LayerKey`. Prefer matching variant (Leaf→Leaf, Zone→Zone) if possible.

+1. **Sibling in same zone.** Pick the nearest remaining entry where `parent_zone == lost.parent_zone` (using beam/score, omnidirectional — closest in any direction). Prefer a Leaf if the lost entry was a Leaf; prefer a Zone if it was a Zone.
+2. **Walk up parent zones.** If the lost zone is now empty of children, move to `lost.parent_zone` (the enclosing Zone). If that zone has a `last_focused` that's still registered, use it. Otherwise pick the nearest entry in that zone.
+3. **Walk up to layer root.** Keep walking `parent_zone` until reaching `None` (layer root).
+4. **Walk up layer tree.** If even the layer root has no remaining entries, walk `layer.parent`. Use that layer's `last_focused` if still valid.
+5. **No-focus.** If everything above fails, emit `focus-changed { prev, next: None }`. Window has no focus until a user gesture restores it.

The unregister loop then hits `const key = stableKeys.get(id); if (!key) continue;` and silently skips the unregister call — the kernel keeps a stale `RegisterEntry::Zone` under the now-orphaned `SpatialKey`. This isn't just a memory leak: the stale rect remains a beam-search candidate, so `nav.down` from a real card after a delete can land on a phantom placeholder whose moniker no longer resolves to any DOM element, dead-ending focus. Fix: have `usePlaceholderRegistration` track its own `Map<id, SpatialKey>` in `registeredRef` (rather than just a `Set<id>`) so the unregister path doesn't depend on the live `stableKeys` map. +- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx:355-380` — Placeholder rects are computed in document/scroll-content coordinates while real-mounted card rects (reported by `<FocusScope>`'s `ResizeObserver`) are in viewport coordinates, so the two systems disagree once the column has been scrolled. `baseY = scrollEl.getBoundingClientRect().y` is the container's viewport top — that doesn't move when the user scrolls — so a placeholder for task index `i` always lands at `baseY + i * 80` regardless of `virtualizer.scrollOffset`. When the user has scrolled to view items 50-59, task 0's placeholder still sits at `baseY` (which is the visible top edge) while task 50's real card also sits near `baseY` — beam search would see overlapping rects in completely different "rows". Fix: subtract `virtualizer.scrollOffset` from the placeholder y, or compute `placeholder_y = baseY + (i * ESTIMATED_ITEM_HEIGHT) - scrollEl.scrollTop`. +- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx:355-358` — Falling back to `width = ESTIMATED_ITEM_HEIGHT` when `scrollEl` is null mixes axes (height-as-width).
